title: Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia
chief_complaint: My daughter has been tired, pale, and bruising easily for 3 weeks. She's had a fever on and off. She complains that her bones hurt, especially her legs. She's not her active self anymore.

physical_exam.General: Pale, fatigued child. Small petechiae on lower extremities. Multiple ecchymoses at various stages.
physical_exam.Lymphatic: Generalized lymphadenopathy (cervical, axillary, inguinal - firm, non-tender).
physical_exam.Abdomen: Hepatomegaly (liver 4cm below costal margin). Splenomegaly (spleen 5cm below costal margin). No tenderness.
physical_exam.Musculoskeletal: Bone tenderness to palpation of sternum, tibias, and femurs (marrow infiltration). Refuses to walk.
physical_exam.Neurologic: No cranial nerve deficits. CNS 1 (no blasts in CSF).

hidden_data.cbc: WBC 85,000 (hyperleukocytosis), Hb 4.2 (severe anemia), Platelets 15,000 (severe thrombocytopenia). Peripheral smear: 65% lymphoblasts - small, round, scant cytoplasm, no granules.
hidden_data.bone_marrow: Hypercellular marrow (98% cellularity). >90% lymphoblasts (CD10+, CD19+, CD34+, TdT+). Confirms B-ALL (Precursor B-cell).
hidden_data.flow_cytometry: CD10+, CD19+, CD34+, TdT+, CD20-, CD3-. Common B-ALL (good prognosis).
hidden_data.cytogenetics: Hyperdiploidy >50 chromosomes (favorable). t(12;21) ETV6-RUNX1 positive (favorable). No Philadelphia chromosome (t(9;22)). No MLL rearrangement.
hidden_data.csf: No blasts in CSF. CNS 1 (no CNS involvement).
hidden_data.cmp: LDH 950 (tumor lysis - high tumor burden), Uric Acid 10.5 (hyperuricemia - TLS risk), Creatinine 1.2, K+ 4.8, Ca 8.5, Phosphorus 5.8
hidden_data.cxr: Mediastinal mass ABSENT (rules out T-ALL). No pneumonia.

correct_diagnosis: Precursor B-Cell Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia (B-ALL) - Standard Risk (Hyperdiploid, ETV6-RUNX1) with Hyperleukocytosis and Tumor Lysis Syndrome Risk
key_learning_points: ALL = most common childhood cancer (25% of all pediatric cancers). Peak age 2-5 years. B-ALL (85%) > T-ALL (15%). Survival now >90% for standard risk

Presentation: Bone marrow failure (anemia → fatigue/pallor, thrombocytopenia → petechiae/bruising, neutropenia → fever/infections), Bone pain (marrow infiltration - children may refuse to walk), Lymphadenopathy, Hepatosplenomegaly, Mediastinal mass (T-ALL), CNS involvement (<5% at diagnosis)

Risk stratification: Age (1-10 years favorable), Initial WBC (<50K favorable), Immunophenotype (B-ALL better than T-ALL), Cytogenetics (Hyperdiploidy, ETV6-RUNX1 = favorable. Hypodiploidy, MLL, BCR-ABL, iAMP21 = unfavorable), MRD (Minimal Residual Disease) at day 29 - most important prognostic factor

Treatment: 4 phases: Induction (Vincristine, Dexamethasone, PEG-Asparaginase, Daunorubicin), Consolidation (Cyclophosphamide, Cytarabine, 6-MP, Methotrexate), Interim Maintenance, Delayed Intensification, Maintenance (Methotrexate, 6-MP, Vincristine, Dexamethasone pulses - 2-3 years total). CNS prophylaxis: Intrathecal Methotrexate/Cytarabine/Hydrocortisone

Tumor lysis syndrome: High-risk features (hyperleukocytosis, elevated LDH, uric acid, renal dysfunction). Prevention: Aggressive IV hydration (3L/m²/day), Allopurinol or Rasburicase (if uric acid >8), monitor electrolytes q6-8h. Can cause fatal hyperkalemia, hypocalcemia, renal failure

Supportive care: Transfuse platelets for <10K or bleeding. pRBC for Hb <7 or symptomatic. Neutropenic precautions if ANC <500. Prophylaxis: Bactrim (PJP), Fluconazole (fungal), Acyclovir (HSV/VZV). Febrile neutropenia: Cefepime or Meropenem immediately
